coupling.note: No new module coupling. Clock and Duration added to existing 'effect' imports. The Clock service requirement on the layer is transparent (default service provided by all runtimes). Duration.DurationInput is already used in tool.ts Def interface — this is consistent.

debuggability.note: Missing span annotations for three critical edge cases: (1) TTL expiry — 'cache.expired' annotation when an entry is deleted due to TTL, (2) LRU eviction — 'cache.evicted' annotation with count of evicted entries, (3) Size-limit skip — 'cache.skipped' annotation when maxEntrySize prevents caching. Without these, the next developer sees 'tool result not cached' with zero diagnostic breadcrumbs. Also, tool.ts wraps cache calls in Effect.orDie — any cache defect crashes the process.

convergence.note: Single file change with a clear, linear path: Map replacement → TTL check → LRU eviction → size guard → stats counters. The plan acknowledges the Map insertion-order LRU is approximate (not true access-order), which is an honest tradeoff call.

surface_area.note: Only cache.ts changes. Interface is extended with new methods (invalidateKey, stats) but structural typing protects existing consumers. New exports (CacheConfig, CacheStats) are additive. Minor deduction: tool.ts currently annotates cache.hit=true unconditionally after getOrCompute — this will conflict with the plan's accurate hit/miss annotation inside the cache. Recommend removing the duplicate from tool.ts.

testability.note: Clock.currentTimeMillis is a significant improvement over Date.now() — TestClock.adjust works where no mock could. TestClock is already wired in the project's test infrastructure (test/lib/effect.ts). No test plan is included, but the design makes testing feasible. Minor deduction for the inflight dedup remaining as Record instead of Map — a small inconsistency.

error_clarity.note: Three silent fallback paths with no error signal: (1) maxEntrySize exceeded → silently skips caching, (2) LRU eviction → silently drops entries, (3) TTL expiry → silently treats as miss. A future developer investigating 'why is this tool result always slow?' gets zero diagnostics from the cache layer. Need at minimum Effect.annotateCurrentSpan on each path, and ideally Effect.logWarning for size-limit skips.

reversibility.note: Single file change with purely additive interface extensions. Reverting cache.ts restores the original behavior exactly. No other file touched, no import ripple. The Map→Record revert is trivial since both are used identically from the consumer's perspective.

objections.evidence: cache.ts (planned): getOrCompute hit/miss check has Effect.annotateCurrentSpan, but TTL expiry, LRU eviction, and size-limit skip paths have no span annotations. Combined with tool.ts wrap() wrapping cache calls in Effect.orDie, any cache defect crashes the process with no diagnostic trail.
objections.finding: Every silent fallback in the cache (expiry, eviction, size limit) must have an Effect.annotateCurrentSpan call that names the path and includes the key. Developers debugging 'why is this tool result never cached' need span-level diagnostics.

objections.evidence: Planned maxEntrySize check: 'if too large skip caching' — no error, no warning, no span annotation. The compute still runs but the result is discarded. This is invisible to the caller.
objections.finding: When maxEntrySize is exceeded, at minimum emit Effect.annotateCurrentSpan({ 'cache.skipped_size': true, 'cache.entry_size_bytes': size }). Consider Effect.logWarning with structured metadata for observability.

objections.evidence: tool.ts wrap(): line ~191 annotates cache.hit=true unconditionally after cache.getOrCompute. The plan's internal getOrCompute already annotates hit/miss accurately. This creates duplicate span attributes — the outer annotation is always wrong when the cache missed.
objections.finding: Remove the duplicate effect.annotateCurrentSpan({ 'cache.hit': true }) from tool.ts wrap() after cache.getOrCompute. The cache layer owns hit/miss diagnostics.

conditions: Add Effect.annotateCurrentSpan for TTL expiry path with key and reason
Add Effect.annotateCurrentSpan for LRU eviction path with evicted key count
Add Effect.annotateCurrentSpan or Effect.logWarning for maxEntrySize skip with entry byte size
Remove duplicate cache.hit=true annotation from tool.ts wrap() — the cache layer owns this now

follow_up_issues.issue: tool.ts wrap() unconditionally annotates cache.hit=true after getOrCompute — this is misleading when the cache missed. Remove after cache layer self-annotates.
Inflight dedup in cache.ts still uses Record<string, Deferred>. Consider converting to Map for consistency with the main cache.

Plan should describe the integration seam between Def.cacheable ({ttl?, maxSize?}) and CacheConfig. The Def type already has cacheable: boolean | { ttl?: Duration.DurationInput; maxSize?: number } — wire this into getOrCompute's optional config param for per-tool TTL/maxSize overrides.
